what_it_does: Defines a global Icon component and an inline SVG path dictionary (ICON_PATHS) so callers can render a named 24x24 icon with configurable size and stroke.
observations: Implements window.Icon as a function component that looks up raw SVG markup in ICON_PATHS and injects it via dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
Contains roughly 100 hand-authored icon entries informally grouped into workspace icons and small action icons.
Uses a 24x24 viewBox, currentColor stroke, round stroke caps/joins, default size 16, and default stroke 1.5.
Falls back to the 'dot' icon when a requested name is not found.
Exposes window.IconNames as the list of supported icon keys.
Has no imports, exports, or module boundaries; everything is attached to window.
Includes duplicate or near-duplicate entries such as 'x' repeating 'close' and 'cube'/'package' sharing the same paths.
standouts: Large inline SVG path library stored as HTML strings and rendered with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
Global window.Icon registration instead of ES module export.
Self-contained file with no external dependencies.
conventions: Default parameter values for size and stroke.
Rest-prop spread onto the root svg element.
Global window namespace for shared utilities.
concerns: dangerouslySetInnerHTML is used to render SVG strings, bypassing React's normal escaping even though the strings are local.
Attaching components to window complicates testing, bundling, and tree-shaking.
No type checking or prop validation for name, size, or stroke.
Duplicate icon entries increase maintenance surface without adding distinct glyphs.
notes: A self-contained, global icon registry that trades modularity for zero-dependency convenience.
questions: Is this file bundled by a tool that understands JSX, given it has no import of React?
Are icon names expected to come from user input or only hardcoded UI labels?
Why are some icons duplicated rather than aliased by reference?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—inline SVG icons used across the Studio—matches the file's content; it is a global icon component and path dictionary.
apparent_themes: Visual consistency through a unified icon language
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to give the Studio a lightweight, name-driven icon system without relying on an external icon library, while keeping all glyph definitions in one place.
novelty: It is unusual to see a hand-curated SVG icon set stored as raw HTML path strings and injected via dangerouslySetInnerHTML inside a globally assigned JSX component.
contribution: It contributes a comprehensive, stylistically uniform inline SVG icon catalog and a simple name-based rendering component that a fused UI system could keep for consistent iconography.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a global Icon component and an inline SVG path dictionary providing roughly 100 named 24x24 icons for a design studio UI.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to provide a lightweight, zero-dependency, name-driven icon rendering system shared across the application.
